name: Jordan River Basin
depletionRate: Very High — Dead Sea dropping 1 m/year
description: The Jordan River basin is shared by Israel, Jordan, Palestine, Syria, and Lebanon in one of the world's most water-scarce and politically contested regions. The lower Jordan has lost 90% of its historic flow. The Dead Sea is dropping roughly 1 meter per year, with sinkholes proliferating along retreating shorelines. Israel's desalination capacity partially offsets scarcity, but Jordan faces acute shortage exacerbated by Syrian refugee influx.
